Gold.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $4496.30 (-1.9%) vs history prev_close $4570.40 (-3.5%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: Markets sold off hard on Friday, with SPY down 1.9% to $648.57 and QQQ dropping 2.03% to $582.06 — both accelerating below key technical levels as the Fed's hawkish pause thesis reasserts dominance. Per Yahoo Finance, the Fed confirmed one rate cut expected in 2026 with GDP now forecast at 2.4% and core inflation at 2.7% — a 'higher for longer' configuration that continues compressing tech multiples. VIX surged 11.26% to 26.78, and the 10-year Treasury yield climbed to 4.39% (+2.57% on the session), adding fuel to the duration headwind for growth stocks. The US-China tech war escalated further as the Trump Administration extended export restrictions to subsidiaries of already-sanctioned Chinese firms — a move the Times of India reports affects tens of thousands of entities including Huawei and YMTC subsidiaries — injecting fresh supply chain uncertainty ahead of an anticipated Trump-Xi APEC meeting.
commodities_note: WTI crude held firm at $98.53 (+0.45%) as the Hormuz crisis enters its third week — the strait remains near-closed, and per Wood Mackenzie analysis, prices could exceed $100/bbl if tanker flows are not quickly restored. XLE is now overbought (RSI 75.5) and 11.5% above its 50-day average, making new entries unattractive despite the geopolitical bid. Gold shows a data anomaly that must be flagged: the pipeline's fast_info source reports a previous close of $4,496.30 (implying -1.9% to $4,411.60), while the historical data source shows a previous close of $4,570.40 (implying -3.5%). The fast_info figure is used as the headline close. Given this conflict, treat today's gold move with caution — the direction (lower) is consistent across both sources, but the magnitude is uncertain. Structurally, gold remains in a powerful uptrend; with GLD likely 15%+ above its 50-day average at these levels, no attractive entry exists for new positions despite the all-time-high territory.

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — DBS has slipped 1.58% below its declining 50-day average of SGD 57.56 with RSI at 49.5, and the -8.8% expected earnings decline from analyst consensus warrants a downgrade. This is a material concern: negative earnings growth in a rate-pause environment, combined with the NIM compression headwind that has plagued the Singapore bank cluster for three consecutive months, removes the case for holding at current prices. The 5.33% dividend yield is attractive for patient investors, but at 13.7x forward PE — in line with the cluster median — there is no valuation cushion. R:R is unfavourable at 0.5:1 (upside to SGD 58.73 resistance vs. invalidation at SGD 52.53 support). Nearest structural support is the 200-day average at SGD 52.18 (7.9% below). Volume is low, confirming no institutional accumulation. Bottom line: the yield is real, but earnings are declining and the rate environment is working against NIM recovery. Wait for US10Y to break below 4.20% or a confirmed earnings inflection before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to HOLD — OCBC remains the cleanest name in the Singapore cluster, sitting just 0.31% above its rising 50-day average at SGD 21.01 with RSI at 50.4, but the tight invalidation flag demands explicit attention. Invalidation sits at the 50-day average itself (SGD 21.01), only 0.3% below current price — normal intraday volatility could trigger this before the thesis is tested. Use the 200-day average at SGD 18.20 (13.7% below) as the actual mental stop for position sizing. The R:R appears attractive at 11.7:1 on paper (to resistance at SGD 21.81), but the tight stop means the stated ratio is mechanically derived from a whisker-thin buffer. Volume is low — no institutional conviction is visible. The 3.93% yield and 11.8x forward PE (14% discount to cluster median) are supportive, but the PEG of 4.49 is expensive for the 3.7% earnings growth rate. Downgrade from WATCH reflects today's broader market sell-off — the setup needs to prove it can hold the 50-day average under pressure before re-earning a WATCH. Bottom line: OCBC is the best value in the Singapore cluster, but wait for it to demonstrate SMA50 support with volume before entering.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. UOB sits 2.51% below its rising 50-day average at SGD 37.39, with RSI at 44.7 and a -6.7% expected earnings decline — analyst consensus is HOLD, not buy. The PEG of 2.34 is expensive for a business with -1.8% revenue growth, and at 10x forward PE (27% discount to cluster median), the discount reflects genuine fundamental concerns rather than opportunity. R:R is 3.2:1 (invalidation at 200-day average SGD 35.50, upside to SGD 39.49 resistance) — the ratio is acceptable, but the earnings decline trajectory argues against entry. Support at the 200-day average (2.6% below) is the level to watch; a break below SGD 35.50 opens a path to SGD 34.23 support. Would reconsider if earnings growth guidance improves at the May 7 results.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Singapore Airlines is the most structurally concerning name in the cluster — analyst consensus expects a 64.3% earnings decline, which per the earnings decline prominence rule must be stated upfront and weighed against the signal. A 64% collapse in earnings is incompatible with a WATCH or ACCUMULATE signal; the forward PE of 17.2x (25.5% premium to the cluster median of 13.7x) is unjustified given that earnings are set to crater. The 8.41% FCF yield and 5.3% dividend yield provide a cash flow floor, but if the earnings decline materialises, dividend sustainability becomes a question. R:R is poor at 0.7:1 (upside to SGD 6.65 resistance vs. invalidation at SGD 6.34 support). The Hormuz oil crisis remains an active cost headwind — jet fuel is a primary operating expense, and elevated energy costs compound the margin pressure. Avoid until the earnings decline narrative reverses.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Singtel has extended 4.33% above its rising 50-day average of SGD 4.82, holding above the SMA for 35 consecutive sessions, and RSI at 52.4 is neutral-bullish. The signal upgrade reflects a technical improvement over prior CAUTION: the trend structure is intact with the 50-day SMA rising and price holding despite today's broad market sell-off of 2-3%. However, the R:R is poor at 0.6:1 (upside to SGD 5.15 resistance vs. invalidation at SGD 4.82 SMA), and the forward PE of 24.9x (82% premium to the Singapore cluster median of 13.7x) is expensive — the PEG of 3.8 on just 0.9% revenue growth is a valuation red flag. The entry_block is null here but price is 4.33% above SMA50 — within the 2-5% 'slightly extended' zone but approaching the hard block threshold. No catalyst override conditions are met. Bottom line: the trend is clean but the valuation is stretched for a telecom growing revenue below 1% per year — watch for a pullback to the SGD 4.82-4.90 zone before considering entry.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. NVDA dropped another 3.48% to $172.93, now 6.32% below its declining 50-day average of $184.60 with RSI at exactly 38.0 — sitting on the edge of the contrarian gate but not through it. The tight invalidation flag applies: invalidation is at $169.81 (1.8% below), with structural support at $164.30 (5.0% below). Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested; use $164.30 as the mental stop for position sizing. The R:R is 3.7:1 to the nearest resistance at $184.47, and volume is above average — confirming the move is real, not a low-conviction drift. Fundamentally, NVDA is extraordinary: 73.2% revenue growth, 95.6% expected earnings growth, and a 15.5x forward PE (17% below the semis cluster median) — the Motley Fool's 'value play' framing per their March 23 article has merit. The Blackwell Ultra ramp (per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call) with a $78B Q1 FY2027 guide is the core thesis. But the 50-day SMA is declining, macro headwinds are intensifying (US10Y now 4.39%), and the US-China subsidiary export ban adds supply chain friction. Bottom line: NVDA's fundamentals are compelling at this price — it's genuinely cheap for what it is — but wait for the 50-day average to flatten or for price to reclaim $184 on volume before entering.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. AMD sits 6.11% below its declining 50-day average at $214.44, with volume above average confirming the downtrend. The R:R is 2.1:1 — invalidation at the 200-day average ($192.97, 4.2% below), upside to $218.84 resistance — which technically meets the ACCUMULATE ratio threshold, but ACCUMULATE requires caution_source to be 'base_scorer' with no hard block, and RSI at 48 is not oversold. The AMD-Samsung MOU on memory chip supplies and foundry partnership (reported by Insider Monkey, March 22) and AMD's joining the Optical Compute Interconnect consortium alongside NVIDIA and Meta add strategic credibility. The Meta $100B+ GPU supply deal per AMD IR press release validates AMD as a credible NVDA alternative at hyperscale. However, the 50-day SMA is declining, revenue growth (34.1%) is below the forward PE (18.7x) divided by PEG threshold concerns, and the FCF yield of 1.4% means this is a growth story with limited cash floor. The structural support at $158.03 (21.5% below) is the worst-case if the 200-day average breaks. Bottom line: AMD has genuine medium-term upside from the Meta deal and AI infrastructure buildout, but the technical setup argues for patience — wait for a reclaim of the 200-day average at $193 on volume.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 44x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — Intel dropped 5.27% on high volume to $43.87, breaking further below its 50-day average. Analyst consensus expects earnings to decline significantly (no specific growth figure provided), and the 44.3x forward PE is 137% above the semis cluster median — the entry_block soft-caution applies. The FCF yield is negative at -2.06%, meaning Intel is consuming cash, not generating it. The R:R is essentially 0.1:1 (upside to $44.02 resistance just 0.3% away vs. 2.9% downside to $42.58 support) — this is the worst risk/reward on the watchlist. Structural support at $34.82 is 20.6% below, suggesting significant downside if the 18A thesis fails to attract foundry customers. Per the Intel research note, the 18A process is described as 'the foundation for our future,' but as reported in the macro news, the company is waiting for foundry customer commitments before 'unlocking spend' on next-gen processes — a chicken-and-egg problem. ASML earnings on April 15 and Intel's own April 24 earnings are the next major fundamental tests. Bottom line: Intel is expensive on forward earnings, burning cash, and has no near-term R:R support for entry — avoid until either the foundry thesis gets a named customer commitment or the stock pulls back to $34-35 structural support.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML fell 3.78% to $1,317.25 on high volume, now 4.99% below its rising 50-day average — approaching but not yet at the 5% hard block threshold. RSI at 42.2 is neutral-bearish, and the R:R is deeply unfavourable at 0.3:1 — invalidation at the 200-day average ($1,024.20, a full 22.2% below) vs. upside to $1,407.54 resistance (6.9% above). The 200-day average as invalidation is structurally significant: a break there would represent a 22% drawdown and likely signal a fundamental re-rating of the EUV monopoly thesis. ASML earnings on April 15 (23 days away) is the key binary event — the High-NA EUV customer ramp with Intel's 14A process (per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call and Intel investor day) is the thesis driver, but until volume shipment confirmations arrive, this is a wait-and-see setup. The PEG of 2.2 is somewhat expensive for 4.9% revenue growth, though the EUV monopoly justifies a premium. The Simply Wall St. analysis notes 85.3% returns over the past year against the recent pullback — the long thesis remains intact. Bottom line: ASML is the only company in the world that can make the machines needed to build advanced chips — the thesis is durable, but the setup isn't right yet. Watch the April 15 earnings for a High-NA volume confirmation that could turn this into a WATCH.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.6% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=10 (<30).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — MU's completed Q2 FY2026 earnings (record revenue, HBM demand confirmed) have validated the thesis, and the stock now sits 5.56% above its rising 50-day average of $400.60 with RSI at 52.3. However, the entry_block is hard: +5.56% above SMA50 triggers the >5% block, and only 10 sessions above the SMA (below the 30-session sustained trend exception threshold). The pipeline correctly blocks entry here. The R:R calculates at 0.7:1 to nearest resistance ($437.83), with invalidation at the 50-day average itself ($400.60, 5.3% below) — not favourable for new entry. The valuation picture has notable anomalies: PEG of 0.26 appears anomalous against a 4.3x forward PE — verify earnings growth estimates before citing PEG as a primary argument. The 756% expected earnings growth and 196% revenue growth are extraordinary, but FCF yield of 0.61% is thin. Per TheStreet's March 22 report, Micron's CEO issued commentary following the strong earnings beat — characterised as a 'bombshell warning' alongside the beat, suggesting guidance nuance worth watching. Bottom line: MU is the strongest fundamental story on the watchlist right now, but it's extended above its average and the R:R doesn't support entry here — wait for a pullback to the $400-410 zone.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon is 7.02% below its declining 50-day average of $220.87 with RSI at 40.6, and the R:R is 1.7:1 (invalidation at $196.93 support, upside to $219.43 resistance) — below the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE consideration. Volume is above average, confirming the downtrend. The $200B AI capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K) is the core thesis driver, but this same spending is what sent shares down initially on missed Q1 operating income — the capex-to-earnings translation timeline is the open question. At 22x forward PE, valuation is roughly in line with the BigTech cluster median (22.3x) with a PEG of 1.92 — reasonable but not cheap. FCF yield of 1.08% is thin for a company spending at this scale. Per Reuters (cited in Benzinga, March 22), Amazon is reportedly developing a new smartphone called 'Transformer' — interesting product news but not a near-term earnings driver. The 200-day average at $224.81 is above price, acting as overhead resistance rather than support. Bottom line: Amazon's AWS and AI infrastructure thesis is intact, but the stock needs to find support at $197 and rebuild before it becomes attractive — not the right entry point today.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Google dropped 2.28% to $298.79 on high volume, with RSI at 39.6 — approaching the contrarian gate but not through it. The tight invalidation flag applies: invalidation sits at $295.95 (1.0% below). Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use $260.14 (the 200-day average, 12.9% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The R:R is 4.1:1 to $311.07 resistance, but this is from a very tight stop — the structural thesis test is at the 200-day average, not $295.95. The Wall Street Journal (March 22) reports AI is reshaping Google Search and SEO dynamics, and a Morgan Stanley survey cited by Investing.com shows SMBs pivoting toward AI-integrated tools — both are potential headwinds to Google's core search advertising model. The PEG of 2.24 is mildly expensive for 18% revenue growth, and FCF yield of 1.05% is thin. GOOG earnings on April 24 (32 days) and the Google TPU v7 strategy (per Google Cloud Next 2025 keynote) are medium-term catalysts. Bottom line: Google is getting cheaper relative to its earnings power, but the AI search disruption risk is real — wait for the stock to find support above $296 with volume before considering entry.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +31.2% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum dropped 10.02% today — still 31.19% above its 50-day average — and the hard block remains firmly in place: the pipeline correctly identifies this as an extreme extension with no trend exception applicable at this level. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion (March 9) are the structural drivers, and per Lumentum's company event reported by MarketBeat (March 21), management targets $2B quarterly revenue with 1.6T transceiver shipments underway. However, the FCF yield is effectively zero at -0.04%, the PEG of 0.15 appears anomalous against the 47.9x forward PE — verify the earnings growth estimate before relying on it. The 10% single-session drop on high volume is a warning sign that the S&P 500 inclusion buying (which typically exhausts in 3-5 sessions per our learned heuristics) may now be reversing. Structural support at $528.01 is 25.2% below — a meaningful drawdown risk. No entry until LITE pulls back at least to the $528-538 zone.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Coherent dropped 9.29% today on high volume — still 10.68% above its 50-day average — with a hard block in place. The NVDA $2B investment (confirmed via NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) and the multiyear supply agreement announced at OFC 2026 (reported by Simply Wall St., March 20) are confirmed strategic catalysts. However, with 136 consecutive sessions above the 50-day SMA and the hard block active, no entry is warranted regardless of the fundamental quality. The PEG of 0.05 is almost certainly anomalous given a 34.2x forward PE — flag as 'PEG appears anomalous, verify earnings growth estimate.' FCF yield is 0.27%, effectively negligible. The $230.10 support zone (near the 50-day average at $229.16) is the level to watch — a pullback there would bring this back into entry consideration range. Would reconsider when COHR is within 5% of its 50-day average.

XLE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +11.5% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: RSI 76 (>=55).

XLE.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION (caution_trim) to CAUTION — maintained but with updated context. XLE is 11.46% above its 50-day average with RSI at 75.5 (overbought), and the hard entry block remains in force. The Hormuz crisis (now in its third week) is the structural driver, and WTI at $98.53 provides the commodity bid. However, Wood Mackenzie analysis in the macro news warns of a dual supply shock — not just current Hormuz exports, but OPEC+ spare capacity being inaccessible — which could push oil above $100/bbl. This is geopolitical, not structural (driven by a discrete conflict event, not a multi-quarter demand cycle with fundamental re-rating). The R:R is 0.5:1 (derived, not observed) with RSI deeply overbought. ETF fundamentals checks are not applied per the ETF exception rule. No entry until XLE pulls back toward the $53-55 zone and RSI normalises below 65. The approximate XLE price equivalent of a WTI pullback to $85-90 would be roughly $50-53 on XLE based on current spread. Bottom line: energy is the right theme given Hormuz, but XLE is too extended to enter now — wait for the geopolitical premium to partially unwind.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 81x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — Palantir slipped 3.0% to $150.68, breaking just below its declining 50-day average of $151.57, and the entry_block soft-caution on the 81x forward PE (261.9% above BigTech cluster median of 22.3x) cannot be justified without explicit catalyst clarity. The tight invalidation flag applies: invalidation at $148.06 (1.7% below current price). Tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use $119.17 (20.9% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The R:R is 14.2:1 on paper, but this ratio uses a whisker-thin stop — the structural thesis test is at $119.17. The Navy ShipOS partnership with Keel Holdings (per Insider Monkey, March 22) and the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity — not firm revenue) remain thesis supports, and the Pentagon AI momentum noted by Motley Fool (March 22) is constructive. The 70% revenue growth rate is real. But at 81x forward PE with FCF yield of 0.35%, this is a high-conviction or high-risk trade — not a passive entry. The signal downgrade from WATCH to CAUTION reflects today's price action breaking the 50-day average. Bottom line: PLTR's defense AI thesis is genuinely differentiated from commercial tech, but 81x earnings is a lot to pay in a 4.39% yield environment — wait for a reclaim of $152 on volume or a pullback to $140 support.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Would reconsider above $130.24 (nearest resistance) with volume, or on a confirmed SPY reclaim of the 50-day average with broad breadth improvement.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster is uniformly cautious for the 13th consecutive session, with D05_SI downgraded to CAUTION today as the NIM compression thesis deepens alongside the Fed's confirmed higher-for-longer stance. US10Y at 4.39% — now 19bp above the 4.20% reset threshold — moves further in the wrong direction for bank profitability. OCBC (O39_SI) remains the relative standout at its rising SMA50, but the cluster-wide verdict is avoid until the rate environment shifts.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM compression, Fed pause confirmed
singapore.key_development: DBS downgraded from HOLD to CAUTION as US10Y breaks to 4.39%, widening the gap from the 4.20% NIM recovery threshold to its largest level this cycle.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX dropped 2.46% to $332.51, reversing last week's outperformance and confirming the broader risk-off tone is now engulfing even the foundry mid-caps. The cluster divergence between MU (strong fundamentals, blocked entry) and mega-caps (NVDA, AMD, ASML all CAUTION) persists, but today's SOXX sell-off narrows the gap — the sector rotation thesis is under pressure. The new US subsidiary export ban on Chinese tech firms (reported by Times of India) adds a fresh supply chain complexity for the entire semis cluster.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Divergence narrowing — mega-cap CAUTION, foundry thesis intact but technically blocked
semiconductors.key_development: US expands export restrictions to subsidiaries of sanctioned Chinese firms, effective immediately — creates supply chain friction for ASML, NVDA, and AMD with China-adjacent customers.

big_tech.summary: All three BigTech names are on CAUTION with PLTR downgraded today. AMZN and GOOG continue deteriorating below declining 50-day averages, and the hyperscaler capex sustainability debate intensifies as US10Y hits 4.39% — the highest this cycle. The cluster is uniformly avoid-for-entry with GOOG the closest to a structural support test ($295.95) and PLTR's defense AI differentiation the one thesis that could re-emerge as a WATCH on a technical reclaim.
big_tech.thesis_status: Avoid — macro headwinds dominant across cluster
big_tech.key_development: PLTR breaks below its 50-day average as broader tech sell-off overwhelms defense AI momentum narrative, triggering signal downgrade from WATCH to CAUTION.

photonics.summary: Both photonics names dropped hard today — LITE -10.02%, COHR -9.29% — suggesting the S&P 500 inclusion buying exhaustion (typically 3-5 sessions per heuristic) may now be reversing, consistent with the broader risk-off session. Both remain hard-blocked due to extreme extension above their 50-day averages (LITE +31%, COHR +11%). The NVDA $2B investments in both remain structurally confirmed, but profit-taking risk at current levels is the dominant near-term signal.
photonics.thesis_status: Fundamentally intact, technically inaccessible — await pullback
photonics.key_development: LITE and COHR each dropped 9-10% on high volume in a single session — the largest single-day pullbacks since the post-NVDA-investment surge, flagging possible technical exhaustion.

energy.summary: XLE held nearly flat (-0.27%) despite broad market weakness — the Hormuz premium is providing a floor. RSI at 75.5 is deeply overbought and the hard entry block remains in force at 11.46% above the 50-day SMA. Energy is the right macro theme, but XLE is the wrong entry point.
energy.thesis_status: Correct macro call, wrong entry level — overbought
energy.key_development: WTI holds above $98 per barrel as Wood Mackenzie warns of dual supply shock; XLE relative strength versus SPY confirms energy equities are pricing in extended Hormuz disruption.

index.summary: VUAA_L (S&P 500 ETF) sits at $126.16, now 4.33% below its declining 50-day average with RSI at 33.7 — oversold but without reversal conviction. SPY's 1.9% decline today confirms the index break is deepening, and the 200-day average at $127.12 has now been breached on a closing basis, removing a key support level.
index.key_development: VUAA_L closes below its 200-day average at $127.12 for the first time this cycle — a meaningful structural deterioration signal for the index thesis.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM)
interconnected.reason: Direct fab partner for NVDA's Blackwell Ultra and AMD's MI450 — both on the watchlist and the Hormuz/tariff macro is reshaping which foundry relationships get prioritised.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 8-10% from current levels, or after AMD's May 6 earnings confirm MI450 shipment volumes are on track.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix (000660.KS)
interconnected.reason: Primary HBM supplier for NVDA's Blackwell architecture — MU's record HBM earnings confirm the memory pricing cycle that SK Hynix is also riding, creating a direct read-through.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after MU's next earnings update (approximately August 2026) for confirmation that HBM pricing holds above the current cycle peak.

interconnected.name: Broadcom (AVGO)
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC partner for hyperscalers (Google TPU, Meta) — the same hyperscaler capex cycle driving GOOG and AMZN's spending directly flows into Broadcom's networking and custom silicon revenue.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if the hyperscaler capex sustainability debate triggers a 10-12% pullback in AVGO from current levels, bringing it closer to its 50-day average.

active_risks: Iran conflict: Strait of Hormuz near-closed (Day 14+) — WTI approaching $100/bbl, Maersk halted passage per The Guardian, Wood Mackenzie warns of dual supply shock if flows not restored
US-China tech war: export restrictions expanded to Chinese-firm subsidiaries effective immediately — tens of thousands of entities affected including Huawei and YMTC subsidiaries per Times of India
Fed hawkish pause: rates held at 3.50-3.75%, one cut forecast for 2026, core inflation 2.7% — US10Y at 4.39% compressing tech multiples
US tariff escalation: Section 122 15% surcharges (150 days) after IEEPA struck down; China 34% retaliatory tariffs — Russell 2000 outperforming Nasdaq by ~9%
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz crisis keeps XLE overbought at RSI 75.5 — a no-entry zone despite the correct directional call — while simultaneously raising input costs for Singapore Airlines (C6L_SI), deepening that stock's CAUTION. The Fed's confirmed hawkish pause, with US10Y now at 4.39%, is the primary driver suppressing NVDA, AMD, AMZN, and GOOG multiples — all remain CAUTION with declining 50-day averages.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US expanded export restrictions to subsidiaries of sanctioned Chinese firms (effective immediately per Times of India) — directly affects ASML's China-adjacent customers, NVDA's distribution chain, and AMD's supply partnerships; adds fresh friction to the semis cluster.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash, avoid new entries across tech and financials — the combination of a 4.39% 10-year yield, active Hormuz disruption, and fresh Chinese subsidiary export bans creates a three-front headwind that argues for patience over deployment.

events_this_week.event: Market open following broad SPY -1.9% / QQQ -2.03% session — elevated volatility expected
ASML earnings — High-NA EUV volume and China restriction impact key thesis tests
Intel (INTC) earnings — 18A customer commitments and FCF trajectory
Google (GOOG) earnings — search AI disruption impact and TPU v7 revenue
AMD earnings — MI450 Meta deal shipment progress and Q1 revenue vs $9.8B guidance
NVDA earnings — Blackwell Ultra ramp volumes and Q2 FY2027 guide

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: US10Y at 4.39% (highest this cycle) combined with the Hormuz supply shock and fresh Chinese subsidiary export bans create a three-front macro headwind — 14 of 17 tickers on CAUTION signals with no actionable BUY or ACCUMULATE setups.

watch_for_entry.note: Strongest fundamental story on the watchlist (196% revenue growth, HBM demand confirmed). Hard-blocked at +5.56% above SMA50. Wait for pullback to $400-410 zone.
Trend intact above rising SMA50 for 35 sessions, but 4.33% extended and R:R poor at 0.6:1. Pullback entry only.

on_deck.note: 15.5x forward PE with 73% revenue growth — genuinely cheap for quality. RSI at 38 approaching oversold. Three-front macro headwind (Fed, Hormuz, China bans) is the blocker.
